      <text x="250" y="154" text-anchor="middle" fill="#40b0f0" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="600">Current Binary info (name, arch, entry point)</text>

      <rect x="40" y="184" width="420" height="48" rx="8" fill="url(#g3)" stroke="#40b0f0" stroke-opacity=".3"/>
      <text x="250" y="212" text-anchor="middle" fill="#40b0f0" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="600">Current Position (address + function name)</text>

      <rect x="40" y="242" width="420" height="48" rx="8" fill="url(#g4)" stroke="#f0a040" stroke-opacity=".3"/>
      <text x="250" y="270" text-anchor="middle" fill="#f0a040" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="600">Available Tools (comma-separated list)</text>

      <rect x="40" y="300" width="420" height="48" rx="8" fill="url(#g5)" stroke="#50c878" stroke-opacity=".3"/>
      <text x="250" y="328" text-anchor="middle" fill="#50c878" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="12" font-weight="600">Active Skill (skill body, if any)</text>

      <rect x="40" y="358" width="420" height="36" rx="8" fill="url(#g2)" stroke="#e06090" stroke-opacity=".2" stroke-dasharray="4 2"/>
      <text x="250" y="381" text-anchor="middle" fill="#e06090" font-family="Inter,sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="500" opacity=".7">Exploration Addendum (only during exploration mode)</text>
    </svg>
  </div>

  <h3>Shared Prompt Sections</h3>
  <p>Defined in <code>prompts/base.py</code>:</p>
  <ul>
    <li><code>DISCIPLINE_SECTION</code> &mdash; "Do exactly what was asked"</li>
    <li><code>RENAMING_SECTION</code> &mdash; Renaming/retyping guidelines</li>
    <li><code>ANALYSIS_SECTION</code> &mdash; Analysis approach</li>
    <li><code>SAFETY_SECTION</code> &mdash; Safety guidelines</li>
    <li><code>TOKEN_EFFICIENCY_SECTION</code> &mdash; Prefer search over listing</li>
    <li><code>CLOSING_SECTION</code> &mdash; Final reminders</li>
  </ul>
</section>

<!-- ═══════════════ 17 UI LAYER ═══════════════ -->
<section class="section" id="ui">
  <div class="section-num">17</div>
  <h2>UI Layer</h2>
  <p><strong>Files:</strong> <code>rikugan/ui/panel_core.py</code>, <code>rikugan/ui/chat_view.py</code>, <code>rikugan/ui/message_widgets.py</code></p>

  <div class="showcase-gallery">
    <div>
      <div class="showcase-label">Binary Ninja</div>
      <img src="assets/binja_showcase.png" alt="Rikugan in Binary Ninja" loading="lazy">
    </div>
    <div>
      <div class="showcase-label">IDA Pro</div>
      <img src="assets/ida_showcase.png" alt="Rikugan in IDA Pro" loading="lazy">
    </div>
  </div>

  <h3>Event Polling</h3>
  <p>A <code>QTimer</code> fires every 50ms, calling <code>_poll_events()</code>. Dequeues up to 20 events per tick and routes each to the appropriate handler.</p>

  <h3>ChatView Widget Mapping</h3>
  <div class="table-wrap">
    <table>
      <tr><th>Event</th><th>Widget</th></tr>
      <tr><td><code>TEXT_DELTA</code> / <code>TEXT_DONE</code></td><td>AssistantMessageWidget (Markdown rendered)</td></tr>
      <tr><td><code>TOOL_CALL_*</code></td><td>ToolCallWidget (collapsible, syntax-highlighted)</td></tr>
      <tr><td><code>TURN_START</code></td><td>ThinkingWidget (animated dots)</td></tr>
      <tr><td><code>ERROR</code></td><td>ErrorMessageWidget</td></tr>
      <tr><td><code>PLAN_GENERATED</code></td><td>PlanView (step list with status indicators)</td></tr>
      <tr><td><code>TOOL_APPROVAL_REQUEST</code></td><td>ToolApprovalWidget (Allow/Deny)</td></tr>
      <tr><td><code>EXPLORATION_PHASE_CHANGE</code></td><td>ExplorationPhaseWidget</td></tr>
      <tr><td><code>EXPLORATION_FINDING</code></td><td>ExplorationFindingWidget</td></tr>
    </table>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- ═══════════════ 18 THREAD SAFETY ═══════════════ -->
<section class="section" id="threading">
  <div class="section-num">18</div>
  <h2>Thread Safety Model</h2>

  <h3>Architecture</h3>
  <div class="flow">
    <div class="flow-node purple">Main Thread (Qt + IDA)</div>
    <div class="flow-arrow"></div>
    <div class="flow-node pink">queue.Queue</div>
    <div class="flow-arrow"></div>
    <div class="flow-node green">Background Thread (Agent)</div>
  </div>

  <h3>IDA API Marshalling</h3>
  <p>IDA Pro requires all API calls on the main thread. The <code>@idasync</code> decorator marshalls calls via <code>ida_kernwin.execute_sync()</code>. Binary Ninja tools run directly &mdash; BN's API is thread-safe.</p>

  <h3>User Answer/Approval Queues</h3>
  <p>Two <code>queue.Queue(maxsize=1)</code> instances:</p>
  <ul>
    <li><code>_user_answer_queue</code> &mdash; For <code>USER_QUESTION</code> responses</li>
    <li><code>_tool_approval_queue</code> &mdash; For <code>execute_python</code> approval</li>
  </ul>
  <p>The agent waits with <code>queue.get(timeout=0.5)</code> in a loop, checking for cancellation between attempts. The UI thread calls <code>put()</code>. No race condition possible.</p>
</section>

<!-- ═══════════════ 19 ERROR HANDLING ═══════════════ -->
<section class="section" id="errors">
  <div class="section-num">19</div>
  <h2>Error Handling &amp; Retry</h2>

  <h3>Exception Hierarchy</h3>
  <div class="error-tree">
    <span class="root">RikuganError</span><br>
    <span class="branch">├── AgentError</span> <span style="color:var(--text-dim)">&mdash; loop-level errors</span><br>
    <span class="branch">├── CancellationError</span> <span style="color:var(--text-dim)">&mdash; user cancelled</span><br>
    <span class="branch">├── ProviderError</span> <span style="color:var(--text-dim)">&mdash; LLM API errors</span><br>
    <span class="leaf">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;└── RateLimitError</span> <span style="color:var(--text-dim)">&mdash; HTTP 429</span><br>
    <span class="branch">├── ToolError</span> <span style="color:var(--text-dim)">&mdash; tool execution errors</span><br>
    <span class="branch">├── ToolValidationError</span> <span style="color:var(--text-dim)">&mdash; argument validation</span><br>
    <span class="branch">├── MCPError</span> <span style="color:var(--text-dim)">&mdash; MCP protocol errors</span><br>
    <span class="leaf">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;├── MCPConnectionError</span><br>
    <span class="leaf">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;└── MCPTimeoutError</span><br>
    <span class="branch">└── SkillError</span> <span style="color:var(--text-dim)">&mdash; skill loading errors</span>
  </div>

  <h3>Consecutive Error Tracking</h3>
  <p><code>_consecutive_errors</code> counts sequential tool failures. After 3 consecutive errors, tools are disabled for the current turn, forcing the LLM to respond with text instead of looping on broken calls.</p>
</section>

<!-- ═══════════════ 20 LOGGING ═══════════════ -->
<section class="section" id="logging">
  <div class="section-num">20</div>
  <h2>Logging</h2>
  <p><strong>File:</strong> <code>rikugan/core/logging.py</code></p>

  <div class="arch-box">
    <div class="arch-item">
      <div class="ai">&#x1F4BB;</div>
      <div class="at">IDA Output Window</div>
      <div class="ad">IDAHandler, INFO level<br><code>[Rikugan] LEVEL: message</code></div>
    </div>
    <div class="arch-item">
      <div class="ai">&#x1F4C4;</div>
      <div class="at">Debug File</div>
      <div class="ad">FlushFileHandler, DEBUG level<br>flushed + fsynced after every write</div>
    </div>
    <div class="arch-item">
      <div class="ai">&#x1F4CA;</div>
      <div class="at">Structured JSON</div>
      <div class="ad">JSONFormatter, INFO level<br>Append-mode JSONL, machine-parseable</div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <h4>JSON Log Format</h4>
  <pre><code class="language-json">{"ts": 1709500000.123, "level": "INFO", "thread": "Thread-1", "msg": "Subagent started"}</code></pre>
</section>

<!-- ═══════════════ 21 COMMANDS ═══════════════ -->
<section class="section" id="commands">
  <div class="section-num">21</div>
  <h2>Commands Reference</h2>

  <div class="table-wrap">
    <table>
      <tr><th>Command</th><th>Description</th></tr>
      <tr><td><code>/plan &lt;msg&gt;</code></td><td>Enter plan mode: generate plan, then execute step-by-step</td></tr>
      <tr><td><code>/modify &lt;msg&gt;</code></td><td>Enter exploration mode: EXPLORE &rarr; PLAN &rarr; EXECUTE &rarr; SAVE</td></tr>
      <tr><td><code>/explore &lt;msg&gt;</code></td><td>Enter explore-only mode: autonomous read-only analysis</td></tr>
      <tr><td><code>/memory</code></td><td>Show current RIKUGAN.md contents</td></tr>
      <tr><td><code>/undo [N]</code></td><td>Undo last N mutations (default 1)</td></tr>
      <tr><td><code>/mcp</code></td><td>Show MCP server health status</td></tr>
      <tr><td><code>/doctor</code></td><td>Diagnose provider, API key, tools, skills, config issues</td></tr>
      <tr><td><code>/&lt;skill-slug&gt;</code></td><td>Activate a skill (e.g., <code>/malware-analysis</code>, <code>/ctf</code>)</td></tr>
    </table>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- ═══════════════ 22 DATA FLOW ═══════════════ -->
<section class="section" id="data-flow">
  <div class="section-num">22</div>
  <h2>Data Flow Diagrams</h2>

  <h3>Normal Turn</h3>
  <div class="diagram">
    <div class="diagram-title">Normal Turn Flow</div>
    <pre style="background:transparent!important;border:none!important;padding:0!important;margin:0!important;color:var(--text);font-family:var(--mono);font-size:.82rem;line-height:1.7"><span style="color:var(--accent)">User</span> "Explain main()"
  │
  ├─→ SessionState.add_message(<span style="color:var(--accent-5)">USER</span>)
  ├─→ build_system_prompt()
  ├─→ provider.chat_stream(messages, tools, system)
  │     ├─→ <span style="color:var(--accent-3)">TEXT_DELTA</span> "The main function..."
  │     ├─→ <span style="color:var(--accent-4)">TOOL_CALL_START</span> "decompile_function"
  │     ├─→ <span style="color:var(--accent-4)">TOOL_CALL_DONE</span>
  │     └─→ <span style="color:var(--text-dim)">USAGE_UPDATE</span>
  ├─→ ToolRegistry.execute("<span style="color:var(--accent-4)">decompile_function</span>", {name: "main"})
  │     └─→ <span style="color:var(--accent-3)">TOOL_RESULT</span> "int main() { ... }"
  ├─→ SessionState.add_message(<span style="color:var(--accent-5)">TOOL</span>)
  ├─→ provider.chat_stream(messages + tool_result)
  │     ├─→ <span style="color:var(--accent-3)">TEXT_DELTA</span> "This function initializes..."
  │     └─→ <span style="color:var(--accent)">TEXT_DONE</span>
  └─→ <span style="color:var(--accent-5)">TURN_END</span></pre>
  </div>

  <h3>Exploration Mode (<code>/modify</code>)</h3>
  <div class="diagram">
    <div class="diagram-title">Exploration Flow</div>
    <pre style="background:transparent!important;border:none!important;padding:0!important;margin:0!important;color:var(--text);font-family:var(--mono);font-size:.82rem;line-height:1.7"><span style="color:var(--accent)">User</span> "/modify Change score from 100 to 999"
  │
  ├─→ <span style="color:var(--accent)">Phase 1: EXPLORE</span> (subagent)
  │     ├─→ SubagentRunner.run_exploration()
  │     │     ├─→ [subagent uses tools, logs findings]
  │     │     ├─→ exploration_report → <span style="color:var(--accent-3)">KnowledgeBase</span>
  │     │     └─→ phase_transition("plan") → KnowledgeBase returned
  │     └─→ Parent receives KnowledgeBase summary (~1-2KB)
  │
  ├─→ <span style="color:var(--accent-2)">Phase 2: PLAN</span>
  │     ├─→ PLAN_SYNTHESIS_PROMPT + KB summary → LLM
  │     ├─→ Parse plan → <span style="color:var(--accent-2)">ModificationPlan</span>
  │     └─→ User approves plan
  │
  ├─→ <span style="color:var(--accent-4)">Phase 3: EXECUTE</span>
  │     ├─→ For each PlannedChange:
  │     │     ├─→ EXECUTE_STEP_PROMPT → LLM
  │     │     ├─→ Smart patch skill activated
  │     │     ├─→ execute_python (with approval) → patch bytes
  │     │     ├─→ redecompile_function → verify
  │     │     └─→ exploration_report(category="<span style="color:var(--accent-4)">patch_result</span>") → PatchRecord
  │     └─→ All patches applied
  │
  └─→ <span style="color:var(--accent-3)">Phase 4: SAVE</span>
        ├─→ <span style="color:var(--accent-2)">SAVE_APPROVAL_REQUEST</span> → User
        ├─→ "Save All" → write to file → <span style="color:var(--accent-3)">SAVE_COMPLETED</span>
        └─→ "Discard All" → restore original bytes → <span style="color:var(--accent-2)">SAVE_DISCARDED</span></pre>
  </div>

  <h3>Mutation Tracking</h3>
  <div class="diagram">
    <div class="diagram-title">Mutation &amp; Undo Flow</div>
    <pre style="background:transparent!important;border:none!important;padding:0!important;margin:0!important;color:var(--text);font-family:var(--mono);font-size:.82rem;line-height:1.7">LLM calls <span style="color:var(--accent-4)">rename_function</span>(old="sub_401000", new="main")
  │
  ├─→ <span style="color:var(--accent-3)">capture_pre_state()</span> → {} (no pre-state needed for renames)
  ├─→ ToolRegistry.execute("rename_function", {...})
  ├─→ <span style="color:var(--accent)">build_reverse_record()</span> → MutationRecord(
  │     reverse_tool="rename_function",
  │     reverse_args={"old_name": "main", "new_name": "sub_401000"})
  ├─→ _mutation_log.append(record)
  └─→ <span style="color:var(--accent-5)">MUTATION_RECORDED</span> event → UI (MutationLogPanel)

<span style="color:var(--accent)">User</span> "/undo"
  │
  ├─→ Pop last MutationRecord from _mutation_log
  ├─→ ToolRegistry.execute("rename_function",
  │     {"old_name": "main", "new_name": "sub_401000"})
  └─→ <span style="color:var(--accent)">TEXT_DONE</span> "Undone: Rename function main → sub_401000"</pre>
  </div>
</section>

</main><!-- .main -->
</div><!-- .layout -->

</footer>

<!-- ═══════════════ SCRIPTS ═══════════════ -->
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/prism/1.29.0/prism.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/prism/1.29.0/components/prism-python.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/prism/1.29.0/components/prism-json.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/prism/1.29.0/components/prism-markdown.min.js"></script>
<script>
// ── Intersection Observer for section fade-in ──
const io = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
  entries.forEach(e => { if (e.isIntersecting) e.target.classList.add('visible'); });
}, { threshold: 0.08 });
document.querySelectorAll('.section').forEach(s => io.observe(s));

// ── Active TOC highlighting ──
const tocLinks = document.querySelectorAll('.toc a');
const sections = document.querySelectorAll('.section');
const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
  entries.forEach(entry => {
    if (entry.isIntersecting) {
      tocLinks.forEach(a => a.classList.remove('active'));
      const id = entry.target.getAttribute('id');
      const link = document.querySelector(`.toc a[href="#${id}"]`);
      if (link) link.classList.add('active');
    }
  });
}, { rootMargin: '-20% 0px -70% 0px' });
sections.forEach(s => observer.observe(s));

// ── Back to top button ──
const btn = document.querySelector('.back-top');
window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {
  btn.classList.toggle('show', window.scrollY > 400);
}, { passive: true });
</script>
</body>
</html>

```

.layout{display:flex;padding-top:56px;min-height:100vh}

  position:sticky;top:56px;width:var(--sidebar-w);height:calc(100vh - 56px);

}

<div class="content">

<h1>Documentation <span class="jp">六眼</span></h1>
<p class="lead">Everything you need to install, configure, and use Rikugan &mdash; the AI reverse-engineering agent for IDA Pro and Binary Ninja.</p>

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════
     GETTING STARTED
     ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ -->

<h2 id="requirements">Requirements</h2>
<ul>
  <li><strong>IDA Pro 9.0+</strong> with Hex-Rays decompiler (recommended), or <strong>Binary Ninja</strong> (UI mode)</li>
  <li><strong>Python 3.9+</strong></li>
  <li>At least one LLM provider API key (or Ollama for local inference)</li>
  <li><strong>Windows, macOS, or Linux</strong></li>
</ul>

<h2 id="installation">Installation</h2>
<p>Clone the repository, then run the appropriate installer for your target host.</p>

<div class="steps">
  <div class="step">
    <h4>Clone the repository</h4>
    <pre><code class="language-bash">git clone https://github.com/buzzer-re/rikugan.git
cd rikugan</code></pre>
  </div>

  <div class="step">
    <h4>Run the installer</h4>
    <div class="tabs" data-group="install">
      <div class="tab active" data-tab="ida-unix">IDA Pro (Linux/macOS)</div>
      <div class="tab" data-tab="ida-win">IDA Pro (Windows)</div>
      <div class="tab" data-tab="bn-unix">Binary Ninja (Linux/macOS)</div>
      <div class="tab" data-tab="bn-win">Binary Ninja (Windows)</div>
    </div>
    <div class="tab-panel">
      <div class="tab-content active" id="ida-unix">
        <pre><code class="language-bash">./install_ida.sh</code></pre>
        <p style="margin-top:8px;font-size:.85rem;color:var(--text-dim)">Optional: pass a custom user dir path</p>
        <pre><code class="language-bash">./install_ida.sh /path/to/ida/user/dir</code></pre>
      </div>
      <div class="tab-content" id="ida-win">
        <pre><code class="language-bash">install_ida.bat</code></pre>
        <p style="margin-top:8px;font-size:.85rem;color:var(--text-dim)">Optional: pass a custom user dir path</p>
        <pre><code class="language-bash">install_ida.bat "C:\Users\you\AppData\Roaming\Hex-Rays\IDA Pro"</code></pre>
      </div>
      <div class="tab-content" id="bn-unix">
        <pre><code class="language-bash">./install_binaryninja.sh</code></pre>
        <p style="margin-top:8px;font-size:.85rem;color:var(--text-dim)">Optional: pass a custom user dir path</p>
        <pre><code class="language-bash">./install_binaryninja.sh /path/to/binaryninja/user/dir</code></pre>
      </div>
      <div class="tab-content" id="bn-win">
        <pre><code class="language-bash">install_binaryninja.bat</code></pre>
        <p style="margin-top:8px;font-size:.85rem;color:var(--text-dim)">Optional: pass a custom user dir path</p>
        <pre><code class="language-bash">install_binaryninja.bat "C:\Users\you\AppData\Roaming\Binary Ninja"</code></pre>
      </div>
    </div>
    <p>Installers auto-detect the user directory for their host. They create plugin links/junctions, install Python dependencies, and initialize the Rikugan config directory.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="step">
    <h4>Set your API key</h4>
    <p>Open Rikugan in your disassembler, click <strong>Settings</strong>, and paste your API key for your preferred provider.</p>
    <img src="assets/rikugan_settings.png" alt="Rikugan Settings Dialog" class="doc-img" loading="lazy">
  </div>
</div>

<div class="callout tip">
  <span class="callout-icon">&#x1F4A1;</span>
  <div><strong>Anthropic OAuth:</strong> If you have Claude Code installed and authenticated, Rikugan auto-detects the OAuth token from the macOS Keychain. On other platforms, paste your API key manually or run <code>claude setup-token</code>.</div>
</div>

<h2 id="configuration">Configuration</h2>
<p>Rikugan stores its configuration per host:</p>
<div class="table-wrap">
  <table>
    <tr><th>Host</th><th>Platform</th><th>Config Path</th></tr>
    <tr><td>IDA Pro</td><td>Linux / macOS</td><td><code>~/.idapro/rikugan/config.json</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>IDA Pro</td><td>Windows</td><td><code>%APPDATA%\Hex-Rays\IDA Pro\rikugan\config.json</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Binary Ninja</td><td>Linux</td><td><code>~/.binaryninja/rikugan/config.json</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Binary Ninja</td><td>macOS</td><td><code>~/Library/Application Support/Binary Ninja/rikugan/config.json</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Binary Ninja</td><td>Windows</td><td><code>%APPDATA%\Binary Ninja\rikugan\config.json</code></td></tr>
  </table>
</div>
<p>You can edit this JSON file directly, or use the Settings dialog in the UI.</p>

<h3>Config Structure</h3>
<pre><code class="language-json">{
  "provider": {
    "name": "anthropic",
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
    "api_key": "sk-...",
    "api_base": ""
  },
  "max_tokens": 16384,
  "temperature": 0.2,
  "context_window": 200000,
  "checkpoint_auto_save": true,
  "max_tool_result_chars": 8000
}</code></pre>

<div class="table-wrap">
  <table>
    <tr><th>Key</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr>
    <tr><td><code>provider.name</code></td><td>string</td><td>Provider name: <code>anthropic</code>, <code>openai</code>, <code>gemini</code>, <code>ollama</code>, <code>openai_compat</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>provider.model</code></td><td>string</td><td>Model ID (e.g., <code>claude-sonnet-4-20250514</code>, <code>gpt-4o</code>)</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>provider.api_key</code></td><td>string</td><td>API key for the provider</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>provider.api_base</code></td><td>string</td><td>Custom API base URL (for openai_compat or Ollama)</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>max_tokens</code></td><td>int</td><td>Max output tokens per response (default 16384)</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>temperature</code></td><td>float</td><td>Sampling temperature (default 0.2)</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>context_window</code></td><td>int</td><td>Max context window size in tokens (default 200000)</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>checkpoint_auto_save</code></td><td>bool</td><td>Auto-save sessions after each turn (default true)</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>max_tool_result_chars</code></td><td>int</td><td>Truncate tool results beyond this limit (default 8000)</td></tr>
  </table>
</div>

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════
     USAGE
     ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ -->

<h2 id="opening">Opening the Panel</h2>
<div class="table-wrap">
  <table>
    <tr><th>Host</th><th>How to Open</th></tr>
    <tr><td>IDA Pro</td><td>Press <code>Ctrl+Shift+I</code>, or <strong>Edit &rarr; Plugins &rarr; Rikugan</strong></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Binary Ninja</td><td><strong>Tools &rarr; Rikugan &rarr; Open Panel</strong>, or use the sidebar icon</td></tr>
  </table>
</div>

<h2 id="chat">Chat &amp; Multi-Tab</h2>
<p>Each tab is an independent conversation with its own message history and context.</p>
<ul>
  <li><strong>New tab:</strong> Click the <code>+</code> button</li>
  <li><strong>Close tab:</strong> Click the &times; on the tab</li>
  <li><strong>Fork session:</strong> Right-click a tab &rarr; "Fork Session" to create a copy at the current point</li>
  <li><strong>Persistence:</strong> Tabs are tied to the current file. Opening a different database starts fresh tabs; returning restores saved conversations.</li>
</ul>

<img src="assets/binja_showcase.png" alt="Rikugan Chat UI" class="doc-img" loading="lazy">

<h2 id="quick-actions">Quick Actions</h2>
<p>IDA Pro exposes these in right-click context menus. Binary Ninja has equivalent commands under <strong>Tools &rarr; Rikugan</strong>.</p>
<div class="table-wrap">
  <table>
    <tr><th>Action</th><th>Description</th></tr>
    <tr><td><strong>Send to Rikugan</strong></td><td>Pre-fills input with current selection (<code>Ctrl+Shift+A</code> in IDA)</td></tr>
    <tr><td><strong>Explain this</strong></td><td>Auto-explains the current function</td></tr>
    <tr><td><strong>Rename with Rikugan</strong></td><td>Analyzes usage patterns and renames with evidence</td></tr>
    <tr><td><strong>Deobfuscate</strong></td><td>Systematic deobfuscation of the current function</td></tr>
    <tr><td><strong>Find vulnerabilities</strong></td><td>Security audit of the current function</td></tr>
    <tr><td><strong>Suggest types</strong></td><td>Infers types from usage patterns</td></tr>
    <tr><td><strong>Annotate function</strong></td><td>Adds inline comments to decompiled code</td></tr>
    <tr><td><strong>Clean microcode / IL</strong></td><td>Identifies and NOPs junk instructions</td></tr>
    <tr><td><strong>Xref analysis</strong></td><td>Deep cross-reference tracing</td></tr>
  </table>
</div>

<h2 id="skills">Skills</h2>
<p>Skills are reusable analysis workflows. Type <code>/</code> in the input area to see available skills with autocomplete.</p>
<div class="table-wrap">
  <table>
    <tr><th>Skill</th><th>Description</th></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/malware-analysis</code></td><td>Windows PE malware &mdash; kill chain, IOC extraction, MITRE ATT&CK mapping</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/linux-malware</code></td><td>ELF malware &mdash; packing, persistence, IOC extraction</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/deobfuscation</code></td><td>String decryption, CFF removal, opaque predicates, MBA simplification</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/vuln-audit</code></td><td>Buffer overflows, format strings, integer issues, memory safety</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/driver-analysis</code></td><td>Windows kernel drivers &mdash; DriverEntry, dispatch table, IOCTL handlers</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/ctf</code></td><td>Capture-the-flag challenges &mdash; find the flag efficiently</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/generic-re</code></td><td>General-purpose binary understanding</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/ida-scripting</code></td><td>IDAPython API reference for writing scripts</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/binja-scripting</code></td><td>Binary Ninja Python API reference</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/modify</code></td><td>Autonomous 4-phase binary modification (exploration mode)</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/smart-patch-ida</code></td><td>IDA-specific binary patching</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/smart-patch-binja</code></td><td>Binary Ninja-specific binary patching</td></tr>
  </table>
</div>

<h2 id="commands">Commands</h2>
<div class="table-wrap">
  <table>
    <tr><th>Command</th><th>Description</th></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/plan &lt;msg&gt;</code></td><td>Generate a multi-step plan, get approval, then execute step-by-step</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/modify &lt;msg&gt;</code></td><td>Enter 4-phase exploration mode: EXPLORE &rarr; PLAN &rarr; EXECUTE &rarr; SAVE</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/explore &lt;msg&gt;</code></td><td>Read-only autonomous analysis (explore phase only)</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/memory</code></td><td>Show current RIKUGAN.md persistent memory contents</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/undo [N]</code></td><td>Undo the last N mutations (default 1)</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/mcp</code></td><td>Show MCP server health status</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>/doctor</code></td><td>Diagnose provider, API key, tools, skills, and config issues</td></tr>
  </table>
</div>

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════
     ADVANCED
     ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ -->

<h2 id="exploration-mode">Exploration Mode</h2>
<p>Exploration mode is a 4-phase autonomous agent flow for binary modification. The agent explores, plans, patches, and saves &mdash; with user approval at every gate.</p>

<h3>How to use</h3>
<pre><code class="language-text">/modify Change the score constant from 100 to 999</code></pre>

<h3>Phases</h3>
<ol>
  <li><strong>EXPLORE</strong> &mdash; The agent investigates the binary using all analysis tools. It accumulates findings in a KnowledgeBase (functions, hypotheses, data structures). Runs as a subagent with isolated context.</li>
  <li><strong>PLAN</strong> &mdash; The agent synthesizes findings into a numbered modification plan with target addresses, current/proposed behavior, and patch strategy. You must approve the plan.</li>
  <li><strong>EXECUTE</strong> &mdash; The agent applies patches in-memory for each planned change using the platform-specific smart-patch skill. Each patch is verified via decompilation.</li>
  <li><strong>SAVE</strong> &mdash; You see a summary of all patches (count, bytes modified, verification status). Choose "Save All" to persist or "Discard All" to roll back.</li>
</ol>

<div class="callout info">
  <span class="callout-icon">&#x2139;&#xFE0F;</span>
  <div><code>/explore &lt;msg&gt;</code> runs only the EXPLORE phase in read-only mode &mdash; no patching, no plan. Useful for pure analysis.</div>
</div>

<h2 id="plan-mode">Plan Mode</h2>
<p>Plan mode is a simpler two-step workflow: plan first, then execute.</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">/plan Rename all functions in the crypto module with meaningful names</code></pre>
<ol>
  <li>The agent generates a numbered plan</li>
  <li>You approve or reject the plan</li>
  <li>The agent executes each step with progress tracking</li>
</ol>

<h2 id="mutation-undo">Mutation &amp; Undo</h2>
<p>Every mutating tool call (renames, comments, type changes, prototypes) is automatically recorded with a reverse operation. The Mutation Log panel shows the history.</p>
<pre><code class="language-text">/undo        # undo last mutation
/undo 5      # undo last 5 mutations</code></pre>
<p>Supported reverse operations: <code>rename_function</code>, <code>rename_variable</code>, <code>set_comment</code>, <code>set_function_comment</code>, <code>rename_data</code>, <code>set_function_prototype</code>, <code>retype_variable</code>. <code>execute_python</code> calls are marked as non-reversible.</p>

<h2 id="script-approval">Script Approval</h2>
<p>The <code>execute_python</code> tool always asks permission before running. You see the full Python code with syntax highlighting in a scrollable preview, and can <strong>Allow</strong> or <strong>Deny</strong> each execution.</p>
<img src="assets/approval_example.png" alt="Script Approval Dialog" class="doc-img" loading="lazy">
<div class="callout warn">
  <span class="callout-icon">&#x26A0;&#xFE0F;</span>
  <div>The agent can never run the target binary on your machine. Script execution is limited to the disassembler's scripting API (IDAPython / Binary Ninja API).</div>
</div>

<h2 id="mcp">MCP Servers</h2>
<p>Connect external MCP servers to extend Rikugan with additional tools. Create the config file:</p>
<div class="table-wrap">
  <table>
    <tr><th>Host</th><th>Platform</th><th>MCP Config Path</th></tr>
    <tr><td>IDA Pro</td><td>Linux / macOS</td><td><code>~/.idapro/rikugan/mcp.json</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>IDA Pro</td><td>Windows</td><td><code>%APPDATA%\Hex-Rays\IDA Pro\rikugan\mcp.json</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Binary Ninja</td><td>Linux</td><td><code>~/.binaryninja/rikugan/mcp.json</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Binary Ninja</td><td>macOS</td><td><code>~/Library/Application Support/Binary Ninja/rikugan/mcp.json</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Binary Ninja</td><td>Windows</td><td><code>%APPDATA%\Binary Ninja\rikugan\mcp.json</code></td></tr>
  </table>
</div>
<pre><code class="language-json">{
  "mcpServers": {
    "my-server": {
      "command": "python",
      "args": ["-m", "my_mcp_server"],
      "env": {},
      "enabled": true
    }
  }
}</code></pre>
<p>MCP servers are started in background threads when the plugin loads. Their tools appear with the prefix <code>mcp_&lt;server&gt;_&lt;tool&gt;</code>. Set <code>"enabled": false</code> to keep a server configured without starting it.</p>
<p>The MCP client uses <strong>JSON-RPC 2.0 with Content-Length framing</strong> and sends periodic heartbeat pings every 30 seconds to detect unresponsive servers. A server is marked unhealthy if a heartbeat times out; it recovers automatically when pings succeed again.</p>
<p>Use <code>/mcp</code> in the chat to check the health status of all connected servers.</p>

<h2 id="custom-skills">Custom Skills</h2>
<p>Create custom skills in your user directory:</p>
<pre><code class="language-text"># IDA Pro
~/.idapro/rikugan/skills/                                        # Linux / macOS
%APPDATA%\Hex-Rays\IDA Pro\rikugan\skills\                       # Windows

# Binary Ninja
~/.binaryninja/rikugan/skills/                                   # Linux
~/Library/Application Support/Binary Ninja/rikugan/skills/       # macOS
%APPDATA%\Binary Ninja\rikugan\skills\                           # Windows

my-skill/
  SKILL.md             # required: frontmatter + prompt body
  references/          # optional: .md files auto-appended
    api-notes.md</code></pre>

<h3>Skill Format</h3>
<pre><code class="language-markdown">---
name: My Custom Skill
description: What it does in one line
tags: [analysis, custom]
allowed_tools: [decompile_function, rename_function]
mode: exploration
---
Task: &lt;instruction for the agent&gt;

## Approach
1. First step...
2. Second step...</code></pre>

<div class="table-wrap">
  <table>
    <tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr>
    <tr><td><code>name</code></td><td>string</td><td>Display name</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>description</code></td><td>string</td><td>One-line description</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>tags</code></td><td>list</td><td>Categorization tags</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>allowed_tools</code></td><td>list</td><td>Tool whitelist (empty = all tools)</td></tr>
    <tr><td><code>mode</code></td><td>string</td><td><code>"exploration"</code> activates 4-phase exploration mode</td></tr>
  </table>
</div>

<h2 id="memory">Persistent Memory</h2>
<p>Rikugan automatically creates a <code>RIKUGAN.md</code> file in the same directory as your IDB/BNDB. This file persists across sessions:</p>
<ul>
  <li>The first 200 lines are loaded into the system prompt on every session</li>
  <li>The agent can save facts via the <code>save_memory</code> pseudo-tool</li>
  <li>Approved plans from exploration mode are also saved here</li>
  <li>Use <code>/memory</code> to view current contents</li>
</ul>

<h2 id="export">Chat Export</h2>
<p>Right-click a tab or click the <strong>Export</strong> button to save a conversation as Markdown. Tool calls are formatted with language-appropriate syntax highlighting (<code>c</code> for decompiled code, <code>x86asm</code> for disassembly, <code>python</code> for scripts).</p>

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════
     REFERENCE
     ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ -->

<h2 id="tools">Tools Reference</h2>
<p>50 tools are shared across both hosts with identical interfaces. IDA adds 6 host-specific microcode tools; Binary Ninja adds 13 host-specific IL tools for reading, analyzing, and transforming its intermediate language.</p>

<h3>Shared Tools (50)</h3>
<div class="table-wrap">
  <table>
    <tr><th>Category</th><th>Tools</th></tr>
    <tr><td>Navigation</td><td><code>get_cursor_position</code> <code>get_current_function</code> <code>jump_to</code> <code>get_name_at</code> <code>get_address_of</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Functions</td><td><code>list_functions</code> <code>get_function_info</code> <code>search_functions</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Strings</td><td><code>list_strings</code> <code>search_strings</code> <code>get_string_at</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Database</td><td><code>list_segments</code> <code>list_imports</code> <code>list_exports</code> <code>get_binary_info</code> <code>read_bytes</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Disassembly</td><td><code>read_disassembly</code> <code>read_function_disassembly</code> <code>get_instruction_info</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Decompiler</td><td><code>decompile_function</code> <code>get_pseudocode</code> <code>get_decompiler_variables</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Xrefs</td><td><code>xrefs_to</code> <code>xrefs_from</code> <code>function_xrefs</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Annotations</td><td><code>rename_function</code> <code>rename_variable</code> <code>set_comment</code> <code>set_function_comment</code> <code>rename_address</code> <code>set_type</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Types</td><td><code>create_struct</code> <code>modify_struct</code> <code>get_struct_info</code> <code>list_structs</code> <code>create_enum</code> <code>modify_enum</code> <code>get_enum_info</code> <code>list_enums</code> <code>create_typedef</code> <code>apply_struct_to_address</code> <code>apply_type_to_variable</code> <code>set_function_prototype</code> <code>import_c_header</code> <code>suggest_struct_from_accesses</code> <code>propagate_type</code> <code>get_type_libraries</code> <code>import_type_from_library</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Scripting</td><td><code>execute_python</code> &mdash; requires user approval</td></tr>
  </table>
</div>

<div class="table-wrap">
  <table>
    <tr><th>Category</th><th>Tools</th></tr>
    <tr><td>Microcode</td><td><code>get_microcode</code> <code>get_microcode_block</code> <code>nop_microcode</code> <code>install_microcode_optimizer</code> <code>remove_microcode_optimizer</code> <code>list_microcode_optimizers</code></td></tr>
  </table>
</div>

<h3>Binary Ninja-Only Tools (13)</h3>
<div class="table-wrap">
  <table>
    <tr><th>Category</th><th>Tools</th></tr>
    <tr><td>IL Core</td><td><code>get_il</code> <code>get_il_block</code> <code>nop_instructions</code> <code>redecompile_function</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>IL Analysis</td><td><code>get_cfg</code> <code>track_variable_ssa</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>IL Transform</td><td><code>il_replace_expr</code> <code>il_set_condition</code> <code>il_nop_expr</code> <code>il_remove_block</code> <code>patch_branch</code> <code>write_bytes</code> <code>install_il_workflow</code></td></tr>
  </table>
</div>

<h2 id="providers">Providers</h2>
<div class="table-wrap">
  <table>
    <tr><th>Provider</th><th><code>provider.name</code></th><th>Notes</th></tr>

    <tr><td>OpenAI</td><td><code>openai</code></td><td>Standard SDK, all models including GPT-4o and o-series</td></tr>

  </table>

<!-- ═══════════ SCRIPTS ═══════════ -->
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/prism/1.29.0/prism.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/prism/1.29.0/components/prism-bash.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/prism/1.29.0/components/prism-json.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/prism/1.29.0/components/prism-markdown.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/prism/1.29.0/components/prism-python.min.js"></script>
<script>
// ── Tabs ──
document.querySelectorAll('.tabs').forEach(tabs => {
  tabs.querySelectorAll('.tab').forEach(tab => {
    tab.addEventListener('click', () => {
      const panel = tabs.nextElementSibling;
      tabs.querySelectorAll('.tab').forEach(t => t.classList.remove('active'));
      panel.querySelectorAll('.tab-content').forEach(c => c.classList.remove('active'));
      tab.classList.add('active');
      const target = document.getElementById(tab.dataset.tab);
      if (target) target.classList.add('active');
    });
  });
});

// ── Active sidebar ──
const sidebarLinks = document.querySelectorAll('.sidebar a');
const headings = document.querySelectorAll('h2[id], h3[id]');
const sidebarObserver = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
  entries.forEach(entry => {
    if (entry.isIntersecting) {
      sidebarLinks.forEach(a => a.classList.remove('active'));
      const id = entry.target.getAttribute('id');
      const link = document.querySelector(`.sidebar a[href="#${id}"]`);
      if (link) link.classList.add('active');
    }
  });
}, { rootMargin: '-20% 0px -70% 0px' });
headings.forEach(h => sidebarObserver.observe(h));

// ── Back to top ──
const backBtn = document.querySelector('.back-top');
window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {
  backBtn.classList.toggle('show', window.scrollY > 400);
}, { passive: true });

// ── Reveal ──
const revealIO = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
  entries.forEach(e => { if (e.isIntersecting) e.target.classList.add('visible'); });
}, { threshold: 0.08 });
document.querySelectorAll('.reveal').forEach(el => revealIO.observe(el));
</script>
</body>
</html>

```

<!-- ═══════════ FEATURES ═══════════ -->
<section class="sec" id="features" style="background:var(--bg-2);border-top:1px solid var(--border);border-bottom:1px solid var(--border)">
  <div class="container">
    <div class="sec-header reveal">
      <div class="sec-label">Features</div>
      <div class="sec-title">Everything you need for AI-assisted RE</div>
    </div>
    <div class="features-grid">
      <div class="feat reveal"><div class="feat-icon">&#x1F527;</div><div class="feat-title">63+ Native Tools</div><div class="feat-desc">Navigation, decompiler, disassembly, xrefs, strings, annotations, type engineering, microcode/IL read+write, and scripting &mdash; all callable by the agent.</div></div>
      <div class="feat reveal"><div class="feat-icon">&#x1F3AF;</div><div class="feat-title">12 Built-in Skills</div><div class="feat-desc">Malware analysis, deobfuscation, vulnerability audit, driver analysis, CTF solving, and more. Create your own custom skills too.</div></div>
      <div class="feat reveal"><div class="feat-icon">&#x1F916;</div><div class="feat-title">5 LLM Providers</div><div class="feat-desc">Anthropic (Claude), OpenAI, Google Gemini, Ollama for local inference, and any OpenAI-compatible API endpoint.</div></div>
      <div class="feat reveal"><div class="feat-icon">&#x1F50D;</div><div class="feat-title">Exploration Mode</div><div class="feat-desc">4-phase autonomous flow: Explore the binary, Plan modifications, Execute patches, Save with approval gate. Full undo support.</div></div>
      <div class="feat reveal"><div class="feat-icon">&#x1F4CB;</div><div class="feat-title">Plan Mode</div><div class="feat-desc">Multi-step workflows with plan generation, user approval, and step-by-step execution with progress tracking.</div></div>
      <div class="feat reveal"><div class="feat-icon">&#x21A9;&#xFE0F;</div><div class="feat-title">Mutation Undo</div><div class="feat-desc">Every database mutation is tracked with reverse operations. Type <code>/undo</code> to roll back renames, comments, type changes, and more.</div></div>
      <div class="feat reveal"><div class="feat-icon">&#x1F4AC;</div><div class="feat-title">Multi-Tab Chat</div><div class="feat-desc">Multiple independent conversations per file. Fork sessions to branch analysis from a checkpoint. Auto-save across restarts.</div></div>
      <div class="feat reveal"><div class="feat-icon">&#x1F50C;</div><div class="feat-title">MCP Client</div><div class="feat-desc">Connect external MCP servers &mdash; their tools appear alongside built-in ones. Full JSON-RPC 2.0 with heartbeat monitoring.</div></div>
      <div class="feat reveal"><div class="feat-icon">&#x1F6E1;&#xFE0F;</div><div class="feat-title">Script Approval</div><div class="feat-desc"><code>execute_python</code> always asks permission. See the full code with syntax highlighting before allowing execution.</div></div>
      <div class="feat reveal"><div class="feat-icon">&#x1F4E4;</div><div class="feat-title">Chat Export</div><div class="feat-desc">Export conversations to Markdown with syntax-highlighted code blocks. Tool calls formatted with proper language tags.</div></div>
      <div class="feat reveal"><div class="feat-icon">&#x1F9E0;</div><div class="feat-title">Persistent Memory</div><div class="feat-desc">RIKUGAN.md stores facts per binary. Findings persist across sessions. The agent remembers what it learned before.</div></div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

    </div>
    <div class="arch-grid reveal">
      <div class="arch-item"><div class="icon">&#x1F504;</div><div class="title">Agentic Loop</div><div class="desc">Generator-based turn cycle with streaming events</div></div>
      <div class="arch-item"><div class="icon">&#x1F4E1;</div><div class="title">TurnEvent System</div><div class="desc">23 event types for UI communication</div></div>
      <div class="arch-item"><div class="icon">&#x1F9F0;</div><div class="title">Tool Framework</div><div class="desc">@tool decorator, coercion, timeout, categories</div></div>
      <div class="arch-item"><div class="icon">&#x1F50D;</div><div class="title">Subagents</div><div class="desc">Isolated context for parallel task execution</div></div>
      <div class="arch-item"><div class="icon">&#x21A9;&#xFE0F;</div><div class="title">Mutation Tracking</div><div class="desc">Full reverse operation for every mutation</div></div>
      <div class="arch-item"><div class="icon">&#x1F4CA;</div><div class="title">Context Window</div><div class="desc">Auto-compaction at 80% threshold</div></div>
      <div class="arch-item"><div class="icon">&#x1F9F5;</div><div class="title">Thread Safety</div><div class="desc">@idasync marshalling, queue-based answers</div></div>
      <div class="arch-item"><div class="icon">&#x1F4BE;</div><div class="title">Session Persistence</div><div class="desc">Auto-save/restore across restarts</div></div>
      <div class="arch-item"><div class="icon">&#x1F6E1;&#xFE0F;</div><div class="title">Error Recovery</div><div class="desc">Retry with backoff, consecutive error tracking</div></div>
      <div class="arch-item"><div class="icon">&#x1F4DD;</div><div class="title">Structured Logging</div><div class="desc">JSONL + debug file + IDA output</div></div>
    </div>
    <div style="text-align:center;margin-top:36px" class="reveal">
      <a href="ARCHITECTURE.html" class="btn btn-outline">Read Full Architecture Docs</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<script>
// Nav scroll effect
const nav = document.getElementById('nav');
window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {
  nav.classList.toggle('scrolled', window.scrollY > 40);
}, { passive: true });

// Scroll reveal
const revealIO = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
  entries.forEach(e => { if (e.isIntersecting) { e.target.classList.add('visible'); }});
}, { threshold: 0.08 });
document.querySelectorAll('.reveal').forEach(el => revealIO.observe(el));
</script>
</body>
</html>

`webpage/llms.txt`:

```txt
# Rikugan (六眼)

> An agentic AI reverse-engineering assistant that runs natively inside IDA Pro and Binary Ninja.

Rikugan is a plugin that embeds a full agentic LLM loop — with its own tool framework, skills system, context management, and chat UI — directly inside your disassembler. It is not an MCP client or a wrapper; it runs in-process with access to the host's scripting API.

## Pages

- [Home](index.html): Overview, feature highlights, skills list, and provider support.
- [Documentation](docs.html): Installation, configuration, usage guide, commands reference, skills, MCP setup, custom skills, and full tool listing.
- [Architecture](ARCHITECTURE.html): Internal design — agentic loop, TurnEvent system, tool framework, subagents, mutation tracking, and thread safety.

## Key Facts

- **Hosts**: IDA Pro 9.0+ and Binary Ninja (UI mode). Windows, macOS, Linux.
- **LLM Providers**: Anthropic (Claude), OpenAI, Google Gemini, Ollama (local), any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
- **Tools**: 50 shared tools + 6 IDA-only microcode tools + 13 Binary Ninja-only IL tools = 69 total.
- **Skills**: 12 built-in skills including malware analysis, deobfuscation, vuln audit, CTF solving, driver analysis, and binary patching. Custom skills supported via SKILL.md files.
- **Agentic loop**: Generator-based turn cycle — User Message → LLM Stream → Tool Execution → Yield TurnEvents → Next Turn.
- **Exploration Mode** (`/modify <goal>`): 4-phase autonomous binary modification: EXPLORE → PLAN → EXECUTE → SAVE, with user approval gates.
- **Plan Mode** (`/plan <msg>`): Generate a numbered plan, get user approval, execute step-by-step.
- **Mutation Undo** (`/undo [N]`): Every rename, comment, type change, or prototype modification is tracked with a reverse operation.
- **Script Approval**: `execute_python` always shows the full code for user approval before running. Scripts are limited to the disassembler's scripting API.
- **MCP Client**: Connects external MCP servers via stdio + JSON-RPC 2.0 with Content-Length framing. Heartbeat monitoring detects dead servers.
- **Persistent Memory**: `RIKUGAN.md` next to the IDB/BNDB persists findings across sessions. First 200 lines injected into system prompt.
- **Multi-Tab Chat**: Multiple independent sessions per file with fork support and auto-save/restore.
- **Context Management**: Auto-compaction at 80% of configured context window threshold.

## Config Defaults

- `max_tokens`: 16384
- `temperature`: 0.2
- `context_window`: 200000
- `max_tool_result_chars`: 8000

## Built-in Skills

/malware-analysis, /linux-malware, /deobfuscation, /vuln-audit, /driver-analysis, /ctf, /generic-re, /ida-scripting, /binja-scripting, /modify, /smart-patch-ida, /smart-patch-binja

## MCP Config Format

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "server-name": {
      "command": "python",
      "args": ["-m", "my_mcp_server"],
      "env": {},
      "enabled": true
    }
  }
}
```

Tools from MCP servers appear with the prefix `mcp_<server>_<tool>`.

## Installation

Clone the repo, then run the platform installer:
- `./install_ida.sh` (Linux/macOS) or `install_ida.bat` (Windows)
- `./install_binaryninja.sh` (Linux/macOS) or `install_binaryninja.bat` (Windows)

Installers create plugin links, install Python dependencies, and initialize the Rikugan config directory.

## Source

https://github.com/buzzer-re/rikugan
